listings.title: GC Auto-Temp Soldering Station model 12-070 48w 200-900°F Xytronic
listings.body: This is a GC Electronics Auto-Temp soldering station, model number 12-070, designed for precision temperature control. Features a blue plastic base housing the control electronics, a power switch, and a temperature control adjustment dial. The station includes a metal holder for soldering iron, no solder cleaning sponge included. The system includes a nichrome element soldering iron rated for 60 watts and a temperature range of roughly 150C to 450C Manufacturer: Originally manufactured as an OEM model by Xytronic and distributed by brands like GC Electronics. Sold as shown, and functions as designed. CALL OR TEXT show contact info

listings.title: 1991 Ovation Model 1528
listings.body: 1991 Ovation Model 1528 in remarkably good condition with hardshell case. Part of my pandemic Ovation rescue effort. Shallower bowl makes it easier to handle. Local pickup only No checks, money orders, shipping, usual CL scammer nonsense,

listings.title: 1979 Amaretto Di Saronno/ Disaronno Originale Italy 56 proof
listings.body: 1979 Amaretto Di Saronno Stationary Box W/Rose Green Velvet Made In ITALY Disaronno Originale (28% ABV, 56 proof) is a type of amaretto—an amber-colored liqueur with a characteristic almond taste, although it does not contain almonds.  It is produced in Saronno, in the Lombardy region of Italy, by ILLVA Saronno and is sold worldwide. According to the company, the liqueur is an infusion of apricot kernel oil with "absolute alcohol, burnt sugar, and the pure essence of seventeen selected herbs and fruits". The liqueur is sold in an oblong glass bottle designed by a craftsman from Murano. The product was called Amaretto di Saronno (Amaretto from Saronno) until 2001, when it was rebranded as "Disaronno Originale" for marketing reasons. Disaronno can be served neat as a liqueur, on the rocks, or as part of a cocktail mixed with other alcoholic beverages, cola, ginger ale, or fruit juice. It may also be added to hot chocolate and is an ingredient in the Italian variant of an Irish coffee. The amaretto liqueur can also be used in the Italian dessert tiramisu.
